questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The function f(x) = |x| is an even function.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The function f(x) = |x| is symmetric about the y-axis.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The function f(x) = |x| is indeed an even function, meaning f(x) = f(-x) for all x. It is also symmetric about the y-axis, which is the correct explanation for being an even function.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The integral ∫(1/x) dx is not defined in (0, ∞).
questions.reason: Reason (R): The function 1/x is not continuous at x=0.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The function 1/x is indeed not continuous at x=0, but this is the reason why the integral ∫(1/x) dx is not defined in (0, ∞).

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The definite integral ∫[0,1] (2x+1) dx is equal to [x^2 + x] from 0 to 1.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The definite integral ∫[0,1] (2x+1) dx is indeed equal to [x^2 + x] from 0 to 1, which is the correct application of the fundamental theorem of calculus.
